title: The One Market Where Meta's New AI Glasses Can't Be Sold
summary:

title: "Lord, What Fools These Mortals Be!" Shakespeare's Birthplace To Be "Decolonized"
summary:
"Lord, What Fools These Mortals Be!" Shakespeare's Birthplace To Be "Decolonized"

            Authored by Jonathan Turley,

In Hamlet, William Shakespeare famously wrote, “To thine own self be true.”

The problem is when others want to present a different “truth” long after you are gone. 



Shakespeare is under an unrelenting attack in the United Kingdom from trigger warnings to censoring his prose.

Now, Shakespeare’s Birthplace Trust has announced that it will “de-colonise” the Bard.

title: Germany's Economy At The Point Of No Return
summary:
Germany's Economy At The Point Of No Return

            Submitted by Thomas Kolbe

If anyone still needed a concrete figure to illustrate the dramatic state of the German economy, the Federal Statistical Office has now delivered it. The country’s investment ratio is negative, as depreciation exceeds nominal investments. Slowly but surely, the lights are going out.

Public discourse in Germany often sounds monocaudal and lacks complexity. Regardless of which social conflicts, administrative diff

title: 'Guard Your Mind': The Techno-Libertarian Manifesto
summary:
'Guard Your Mind': The Techno-Libertarian Manifesto

            Via zeptabot substack,

Introduction

The modern nation-state is not natural nor permanent. It’s a technological product of the industrial era’s logic of mass warfare, bureaucracy, and centralized taxation. That logic is breaking down. In a world of cyberspace, mobile capital, and digital commerce and geography, brute force lose much of their leverage. title: Adonis Scores $40M for AI Platform Aiming to Boost Hospital Revenue
summary:
Adonis raised a $40 million Series C funding round to help hospitals use AI to tackle denied and underpaid claims. Its platform automates manual revenue cycle tasks and provides real-time insights, which allows staff to focus on higher-value work. Customers include Mount Sinai Health System, Baptist Health South Florida, AdventHealth and ApolloMD.

summary:

Terns Pharmaceuticals’ lead product candidate, in development for treating a type of leukemia, could offer dosing and efficacy advantages over the Novartis drug Scemblix.
